Wu's Fighting Gauntlets  
 

Quest Item
Slot: HANDS
AC: 4
WT: 0.1 Size: SMALL
Class: MNK BST
Race: HUM BAR ELF TRL OGR IKS VAH FRG DRK
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)
Wu's Fighting Gauntlets
Quest Item
Slot: HANDS
AC: 4
WT: 0.1 Size: SMALL
Class: MNK BST
Race: HUM BAR ELF TRL OGR IKS VAH FRG DRK
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)

Item Type:Armor
Appearance:Monk
Tint:
 
Color (RGB):0, 0, 0
Light:Greater Lightstone
Stackable:No
Merchant Value:10 pp 9 gp 5 sp 0 cp
Tribute:34
Lucy Entry By:Kerasota
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2021-12-13 07:44:38
Page Updated:Wed Jun 7th, 2017

Expansion: Original
Average Price: 35pp Pricing Data...
Rarity: Uncommon
Level to Attain: 16
[Quests | Comments ]

Crafted: This item is crafted by players.
  • Wu's Fighting Gauntlets (Tailoring, 88 trivial)
    • Class: ALL
    Expansion: Original
    Yield: 1 x Wu's Fighting Gauntlets

Quests

This item is used in quests.
Expansion List - Premium only.

West Freeport
Quest Name
Apprentice Tailor Test
Crafted by the tailoring skill, and an Enchanter


Uploaded November 27th, 2008
Send a correction
Post Comment
Stats?
QuoteReply
# Jun 17 2002 at 9:32 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592344474330
Sage
3,705 posts
Don't the Wu's gloves add stats, like the other pieces?
▲ Page top
RE: Stats?
QuoteReply
# Jun 18 2002 at 1:57 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591750195138
Scholar
43 posts
No, they don't. However, the reason the GLS is in the recipe is that these gloves act as a greater lightsource. So you get magic gloves and bright light in one package :)

btw, the Wu's gloves were added to the game a good 6 months or more before any other piece of Wu's. While the other pieces have +agi, VI didn't bother (either accidently or purposefully) to go back and modify the gloves.
▲ Page top
RE: Stats?
QuoteReply
# Feb 20 2003 at 4:00 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591782802097
21 posts
6 months? more like two years dude. Wu's Fighting Gauntlets were out in the first EQ, BEFORE KUNARK, as I recall, wu's armor didn't come out until almost luclin, so there's a MAJOR difference. Time was, this was WAY KUEL. You got a major upgrade from netted that you need to hit for magic so you could drop the willowisps that require magic to hit for the GLS to make the bloody things. It was better than cured and had light source. Since the ONLY race that could be monk at the time was human, so you needed one. That was also the days back before Luclin and Kunark when monks actually used their BARE HANDS instead of relying on wepons as they do now.

They DO need to update it so it gets stats just like the other Wu's pieces, though, just to make it fair.
▲ Page top
RE: Stats?
QuoteReply
# Jan 09 2003 at 12:32 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591770631220
Sage
85 posts
they are also a magic item, allowing you to hit some mobs that otherwise couldn't be hit. (like wisps.)
▲ Page top
Poison Vial
QuoteReply
# Mar 12 2002 at 7:25 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591776661151
3 posts
The poison vile can be made by a number of steps BUT, you can skip all the steps (potery makind and stuff hehe) and just buy the poison vial from a Dark Elf in West Ereeport underground tunles. So you can skip all the ingredints and go buy the pearl and poison vial and cast you spell thicken mana and WaLa Vial of Viscous Mana. I have made 6 vial's at lvl 12 with a skill of 38. I did not fail once
Dark Elf Enchanter
13th lvl
Megann
Cazic Thule
▲ Page top
RE: Poison Vial
QuoteReply
# Jun 17 2002 at 9:29 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592344474330
Sage
3,705 posts
good information.

It's Voila (not WaLa) by the way.
▲ Page top
Vial's of Viscous Mana
QuoteReply
# Mar 12 2002 at 7:12 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591776661151
3 posts
I was a lvl 12 Enchanter when I made this vial of viscous mana my skill lvl was 38. I baught the poison vial from the dark elf down in the tunnles of West Freeport. I got the pearl and cast the spell thicken mana and there I had it the vial. Noe what I do not understand is what the vial is used for well I am guessing it is used for Wu's bracers, but I might be wrong. If I am please feel free to correct me.
Megann Dark Elf
Enchanter 13th lvl
Cazic Thule
▲ Page top
lvl 16 reason
QuoteReply
# Nov 12 2001 at 5:56 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591771409089
10 posts
The reason they say lvl 16 for this is not because of the GLS but because you need an enchanter that's lvl 16 to enhcant the vial of viscous mana for it. Just FYI, all the mana vial spells are in the brownie camp in LFay.
▲ Page top
RE: lvl 16 reason
QuoteReply
# Dec 27 2001 at 12:07 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591758061644
185 posts
actually, it is a lvl 12 spell. cast it with my lvl 14 enchanter alt so my main could make Wu's armor. check out http://www.eqtraders.com for all the info on all the tradeskills
▲ Page top
Maybe its the wisps
QuoteReply
# Nov 12 2001 at 10:12 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592251246757
Scholar
51 posts
I've heard alot of disscussion on the lvl needed according to the site to have these things... I'm gonna guess its cause you have to get a greater lightstone to make them and greater lightstones drop off of willow wisps. So, the lvl requirement might very well be for the person making the gaunts not the wearer... cause any lvl can wear them.

Wilil Ironbear; senceable shaman of the north ;-)
▲ Page top
Level
QuoteReply
# Jul 25 2001 at 8:29 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591763949450
11 posts
Yes you can use these as a monk from level 1+. The same goes for all wu's armor. I'm not sure why someone put level 16 to attain this.
▲ Page top
RE: Level
QuoteReply
# Aug 15 2001 at 12:56 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592248837442
95 posts
cause u need a lvl 12 enchanter for some pieces and a tailor skill of 100 or so...
▲ Page top
RE: Level
QuoteReply
# Oct 16 2001 at 4:00 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
no its not. look at every other piece of wu's. you still need a lvl 12 enchanter for those. as far as tailoring skill, these trivial at 88, where as all the other wu's items trivial at 128-158 depending on the type. you need a GLS to make these, that may be why it says lvl 16, but i doubt it. i think its just a mistake.
▲ Page top
Level Requirement?
QuoteReply
# Jul 06 2001 at 9:42 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592249319406
3 posts
"Level to Attain: 16"

Does this mean that a monk can't use this until level 16? Just curious as I have a monk and wanted to get these. The character hasn't attained 16 yet though.
▲ Page top
RE: Level Requirement?
QuoteReply
# Mar 25 2002 at 2:50 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592253981528
4 posts
What Gael said. You also have to take into account that as a monk you get "magical attacks" which allow you to attack INVULNERABLE! creatures like willowisps with your bare hands and feet(Tiger Claw and Round Kick come to mind). Still, the added AC and Greater Light Stone glow for human monks is a definate plus! I mean, I'm not complaining that my monk Rontor has'em... =)

Edited, Mon Mar 25 14:48:02 2002
▲ Page top
RE: Level Requirement?
QuoteReply
# Sep 20 2001 at 8:37 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592341837046
View User Forum
Scholar
582 posts
No, you can equip them from level 1, but it's a little bit pointless... the real value of these gloves is that it lets you punch creatures that are invulnerable to normal weapons. However, a level 1 monk wearing these will last about 5 seconds against a will-o-wisp -- just because you can damage it doesn't mean you'll demolish it.
▲ Page top
High Prices
QuoteReply
# Jun 18 2001 at 7:14 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591754601941
41 posts
I bought The tunic, sleeves, cap and gorget all for 65pp today, you can get them in the stores around freeport and qeynos for 20-40pp, The tunic sells for 40pp and the gorget for 20pp. Why pay people 50-60pp per piece? when you can buy in stores for half that? BTW I bought them from a player who was a master Tailor. Just because it's new doesn't mean you have to pay out the **** for them.

Lhung Zhe
Monk Prexus server
▲ Page top
RE: High Prices
QuoteReply
# Jul 04 2001 at 6:55 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591761919881
Scholar
22 posts
I wish I was that lucky, you can only buy crafted stuff from a vendor if a player sold it to the vendor. That usually happens when someon eneed some quick cash and sells stuff to buy stuff.

19th Season Wood-Elf Ranger

Human Only?
QuoteReply
# May 29 2001 at 5:34 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
The database lists this item as useable by both Humans and Iksar. However, the picture seems to indicate it's human only.

Which is correct?
▲ Page top
All monks
QuoteReply
# Jun 01 2001 at 3:56 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592341837046
View User Forum
Scholar
582 posts
The picture is from the Pre-Kunark day -- the Gauntlets were put in about 2-3 months before Kunark launched. They can definitely be worn by Iksar as well as Humans.
▲ Page top
Wu's Fighting Armor TAILORED NEW!!!
QuoteReply
# May 24 2001 at 4:16 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592246577615
65 posts
Keep an eye out for your local mastor tailor to be making some of this juicy armor. Pieces trivial past 150 in tailoring so it might be a while before you can get some cheap. I'm a mastor tailor on Brell with a skill of 122 as of 5/24/01 and I made a set for my monk, other then that I wont sell any until I'm trivial on the tunic.
I'm curious how much these would sell for, unless I hear anything different I'm going to sell them for 3pp per ac. Go check out www.eqtraders.com for the stats.
enjoy!
▲ Page top
RE: Wu's Fighting Armor TAILORED NEW!!!
QuoteReply
# Jun 01 2001 at 9:24 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
How do you make wu's fighting armor?
▲ Page top
RE: Wu's Fighting Armor TAILORED NEW!!!
QuoteReply
# Oct 19 2001 at 11:45 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591765525919
Scholar
748 posts
To tailor wu's fighting armor, all pieces require a vial of viscous mana, 4 heady kiolas, an appropriate pattern, and a certain number of silk swatches, based on the item. (Gauntlets also require a greater lightstone). The number of swatches are:

gorget, cap, mask, bracer, mantle, gauntlets - 1 swatch
belt, boots, sleeves - 2 swatches
tunic, pants, cloak - 3 swatches
▲ Page top
RE: Wu's Fighting Armor TAILORED NEW!!!
QuoteReply
# Jun 01 2001 at 3:53 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592341837046
View User Forum
Scholar
582 posts
3pp per AC will result in swift bankruptcy, especially at your current skill. Check the items I'm selling to see the prices I'll sell for on Brell.
▲ Page top
Vial
QuoteReply
# May 11 2001 at 2:51 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Poison Vial also found on Nubs Blackgranite in the Windmill in the Lake of Ill Omen. 1pp max to buy but i dont remember for sure so it could be 1cp but i KNOW its not over 1pp
▲ Page top
RE: Vial
QuoteReply
# Oct 19 2001 at 11:48 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591765525919
Scholar
748 posts
Anwyhere from 1 to 2pp, depending on your charisma and faction. This is where I buy all my poison vials (although now I have my pottery almost high enough to make my own, I may stop visiting Nubs pretty soon).
▲ Page top
RE: Vial
QuoteReply
# Nov 12 2001 at 10:06 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592251246757
Scholar
51 posts
Not sure if this is relivant... but I would still buy the vials for the 1-2pp. True, they can be made. True it took me all of 54 minutes (someone timed me) to go from pottery skill of 15 to trivial on vials. (and less than 50pp for the mastery as well but I'm done bragging now) However!!! ;-) I find it a pain in the kilt to have to buy unstackable clay, tote it to the kiln, buy water and sketches and whatnot and potter something if I'm already in the Ill omen area. It saves alot of time to just buy them and time is as they say, money.


(side note on the vials and wu's... if you want to be the trade skill mac daddy and make everything yourself, I'd start with a trip to Erud's crossing. Its a little out of the way but a port to toxx or Nk will land ya close. On this normally under hunted island, you can find most of the huntable componets for wu's gaunts. Zombie skins to make the vials from the zombies in the water, spiders and willow wisps gallore on the island its self. Wu makers paradice, and you can buy the pearls to make the mana at eather side of the boat ride, Eruden or Qeynos.)

Wilil Ironbear; Northman Shaman currently of the 36th season.

May the 6 hammers guide you and hopefully not hit your thumb.
▲ Page top
Recipe
QuoteReply
# Apr 30 2001 at 6:12 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592245706435
2 posts
1 Silk Swatch - tailored from 2 Spider Silk
1 Glove Pattern - purchase from vendor
4 Heady Kiolas - brewed, 2 Kiola Sap, Flask of Water and Bottle, trivial at 46
1 Greater Lightstone - loot from wisps or purchase (8-10 pp?)
1 Vial of Viscous Mana - Created with 12th level Enchanter spell Thicken Mana. Spell sold at brownie village in Lesser Faydark on Faydwer. Spell components are 1 Poison Vial and 1 Pearl.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: May 28 2001 at 7:20 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Skip the lightstone. It should not be in this recipe.
RE: Recipe
QuoteReply
# Oct 16 2001 at 4:05 PM Rating: Good
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
eh, don't skip the greater lightstone, it IS in this recipe.

Scholar
582 posts
The rest of the set was apparently added last patch. I'd expect 50pp or more per piece initially, since the Gi is AC 9 +4 AGI wt 0.1 -- there are now also magic slippers (for Instill Doubt vs those Invulnerable-without-magic creatures).

▲ Page top
RE: Visous Mana?
QuoteReply
# Feb 20 2001 at 2:49 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
You will need an enchanter for this one. The thichen mana spells is sold in the brownie village. The enchanter will also need a pearl and a poison vial(made by pottery skill) as components.
Yzrael of Brell
